pi_items.source: Task setup scripts confirm this path is created and used as the output location

pi_items.verified_value: Multiparameter Monitor is a real device type supported by OpenICE
pi_items.source: OpenICE documentation and research papers confirm multiparameter monitors are supported devices in the platform

pi_items.verified_value: Pulse Oximeter is a real device type supported by OpenICE
pi_items.source: GitHub mdpnp/mdpnp tree shows Nonin pulse oximeter adapter; multiple sources confirm pulse oximetry support

pi_items.verified_value: Infusion Pump is a real device type supported by OpenICE
pi_items.source: OpenICE documentation mentions Alaris infusion pumps and ColeParmer IV pump simulators; confirmed in version 2.0 release notes

pi_items.metadata_value: 36-character Unique Device Identifier in UUID format (e.g., '550e8400-e29b...')
pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE uses unique device identifiers (UDIs), but format may vary. One example found: 'OC2rcpaYQdOQnLgTvRH0ZCYya91i71pPsxjK' (42 chars, alphanumeric)
pi_items.source: OpenICE documentation references unique_device_identifier as randomly assigned device IDs consisting of numbers and letters; found example in Supervisor Demo Applications Manual reference

pi_items.metadata_value: UDI can be found in device detail view or system logs at '/home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log'
pi_items.verified_value: /home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log is configured as the standard OpenICE logging location
pi_items.source: Setup scripts (setup_openice.sh) configure logging to this exact path; task setup records initial log offset

pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE is a real, open-source Integrated Clinical Environment (ICE) implementation from MD PnP at Mass General Hospital
pi_items.source: GitHub https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp and official website https://mdpnp.org/ and https://www.openice.info/

pi_items.verified_value: All data is synthetic - generated from simulated medical devices within OpenICE, not from external data sources
pi_items.source: Task requires 'commission three simulated medical devices' - devices are created within OpenICE simulator, not loaded from external files or APIs

privileged_info_summary: The task involves OpenICE, a real open-source medical device interoperability platform from MD PnP. It requires creating three simulated medical devices (Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, Infusion Pump) and recording their unique device identifiers in a CSV file. All data is synthetic, generated from simulated devices within OpenICE. The system logs to /home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log. OpenICE is confirmed to support all three device types mentioned. However, the claimed UDI format of 36-character UUID appears contradicted by documented examples showing 42-character alphanumeric identifiers.
